title: Earth Day 2026: Five Ocean Conservation Wins Mark 'Historic Year' for Marine Protection
detail: On Earth Day 2026 (April 22), conservation groups celebrated a series of ocean protection milestones achieved over the preceding year. The Pew Charitable Trusts highlighted five major achievements: the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies entering into force in September 2025 (the first binding international agreement targeting harmful fishing subsidies); the BBNJ High Seas Treaty becoming binding international law on January 17, 2026; Australia's national ocean protection commitment to safeguard 30% of its ocean territory in highly protected marine sanctuaries by 2030; the recovery of green turtle populations from near-extinction to 'least concern' status; and global ocean protection crossing 10% for the first time. Environmental advocates noted that while progress has been unprecedented — driven by Portugal and Chile implementing substantial new MPAs — the pace must tripled to reach the Kunming-Montreal 30x30 target by 2030.

media.caption: Earth Day 2026: Five major ocean conservation wins highlight a historic year for marine protection

title: MSC Foundation's Ocean Cay Marine Conservation Center Marks One-Year Milestone on Earth Day
detail: The MSC Foundation marked the first anniversary of its Marine Conservation Center at Ocean Cay, MSC Marine Reserve in the Bahamas on Earth Day, April 22, 2026. In its inaugural year, the center trained more than 20 marine science students, engaged over 17,000 visitors in hands-on ocean education programs, grew over 600 coral fragments in an underwater nursery, and outplanted more than 250 corals to reef restoration sites around Ocean Cay. The facility serves as a model for how commercial maritime operations can directly fund and conduct meaningful marine conservation science and community education at scale.

media.caption: MSC Foundation's Ocean Cay Marine Conservation Center marks its first year with 250+ corals outplanted to reef restoration sites
